changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present and valid. work_id=LC-OBS2-QUESTIONNAIRE-SEMANTICS-INVESTIGATION, branch=feature/lc-obs2-questionnaire-semantics-investigation, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=MIXED. HIGH is correct: investigation may result in a code change touching ScoringEngine lifestyle overlay activation path — an Intelligence Core boundary. MIXED is correct: investigation note is CONTENT; any mapper/orchestrator/scoring fix is BEHAVIOUR. TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH required for HIGH risk.

Both OBS-2 preservation files confirmed and read. Completion note at lines 50-53: 'AB band label diverges between baseline (no questionnaire) and statin_off (explicit None). Requires GPT/human confirmation on whether states are expected to differ.' PROVING_REPORT.md confirms: AB baseline consumer_band_labels=['strong','stable','review'], AB statin_off=['strong','watch','review']. The second band diverges (metabolic/blood_sugar domain). VR statin_off/statin_on also diverge from VR baseline on second and third bands.

Root cause fully traced through source content. Five-step causal chain: (1) orchestrator.py:271 `if questionnaire_data:` — dict {'long_term_medications': ['None']} is truthy, so map_submission runs; None is falsy, so baseline skips entirely. (2) questionnaire_mapper.py:310-336 _map_exercise_minutes — no vigorous_exercise_days or resistance_training_days keys present in statin_off payload → returns 0 (hardcoded default, not null). (3) These lifestyle_factors (exercise_minutes_per_week=0 among other defaults) are injected into user_data at orchestrator.py:325-331. (4) orchestrator.py:1306 passes context.user.lifestyle_factors (non-empty dict) as lifestyle_data to score_biomarkers. (5) overlays.py:380-388 _determine_exercise_level(0) → VERY_POOR; overlays.py:157-162 adjustment_factor=0.8; the metabolic/blood_sugar score is multiplied by 0.8, dropping it from stable band (>=65) to watch band (>=45). Baseline receives no overlay — lifestyle_data={} is falsy at orchestrator.py:536.

First point of semantic divergence is orchestrator.py:536 `if lifestyle_data:`. For baseline this is falsy ({}). For statin_off this is truthy (non-empty dict from map_submission defaults). The upstream cause is orchestrator.py:271 gate which triggers map_submission for any non-None questionnaire_data. The proximate scoring divergence is the exercise_minutes_per_week=0 default injected by _map_exercise_minutes when exercise questions are absent — this activates a VERY_POOR exercise adjustment (0.8 multiplier) that baseline never receives.

Two mapper paths exhibit different semantics for absent fields. Legacy path (_map_exercise_minutes, lines 310-336): returns 0 when no exercise questions are answered — treats absence as 'zero exercise'. Newer path (extract_behavioural_lifestyle_inputs, lines 541-588): emits only keys for questions that are actually present — treats absence as 'unknown, do not inject'. This inconsistency means a partial questionnaire (any non-None payload) activates the legacy path's sentinel defaults, while no questionnaire skips them entirely. This is the behavioural inconsistency underlying OBS-2.

statin_off scenario confirmed at lines 22-26: questionnaire_data = {long_term_medications: ['None']}, lifestyle_fixture: null. This is the exact payload that triggers the OBS-2 divergence. AB panel user dict confirmed at backend/tests/fixtures/panels/ab_full_panel_with_ranges.json:794-797: only age=58 and biological_sex=male — no lifestyle inputs — so all lifestyle overlay activation comes exclusively from the questionnaire path.

PROVING_REPORT confirms statin_off vs statin_on invariant PASSES for both AB and VR. Both statin_off and statin_on have identical questionnaire structure (only long_term_medications differs), so both receive the same lifestyle_factor defaults from map_submission, including exercise_minutes_per_week=0. The band labels are identical between them. This invariant must remain PASS after any fix.

Any code fix touches Intelligence Core. The three candidate fix surfaces are: (A) questionnaire_mapper.py _map_exercise_minutes — change from returning 0 to returning None when no exercise questions answered, then filter None values from lifestyle_factors; (B) orchestrator.py:1306 score_biomarkers call — add a gate so lifestyle_data is only passed when lifestyle-relevant fields (exercise, diet, alcohol, sleep) were explicitly answered; (C) orchestrator.py:271 map_submission gate — change to only run map_submission for fields relevant to medical context (medications), not lifestyle scoring. All three options change emitted scores for partial questionnaire inputs. Cursor must not implement without explicit GPT/human classification of which fix is product-correct.

Prompt includes SOP preflight checks (branch, token, working tree). Guardrails at lines 159-165: do not assume difference=bug; do not widen into general questionnaire semantics; do not alter statin-path behaviour unless OBS-2 root cause requires it. Investigation method (sections A-E) is well-bounded: reproduce, trace, establish intended semantics, decide, add test. STOP condition implied by acceptance criterion 1: OBS-2 must be reproducibly explained before any fix is attempted.

Scope explicitly excludes: broad questionnaire redesign, multi-medication expansion, statin-path redesign, new proving harness work, broad analytics refactor. No .codex/, AGENTS.md, or IDE artefact creation expected. Investigation note format is defined in prompt (sections 1-8), limiting scope of output.

Any fix to the exercise default sentinel must be regression-tested against: (1) statin_off vs statin_on invariant — must stay PASS (identical bands); (2) full questionnaire submission — exercise_minutes_per_week must still be scored when exercise questions ARE answered; (3) baseline (no questionnaire) — must remain unchanged; (4) lifestyle_context scenario — bands must remain unchanged (lifestyle comes from separate fixture, not questionnaire). The fix must be the narrowest change that equalises the baseline vs statin_off band at the metabolic domain without side-effecting other scenarios.

authority_files_verified.note: OBS-2 recorded at lines 50-53. Read and confirmed.
OBS-2 at lines 169-171. Band divergence data confirmed: AB baseline=['strong','stable','review'], AB statin_off=['strong','watch','review'].
User dict at lines 794-797: only age=58 and biological_sex=male. No lifestyle inputs in fixture.
Questionnaire gate at line 271. Lifestyle_data path at line 1306. LifestyleModifierEngine gate at line 1624.
_map_exercise_minutes at lines 310-336 (returns 0 default). extract_behavioural_lifestyle_inputs at lines 541-588 (emits only present keys).
_determine_exercise_level at lines 380-388. VERY_POOR adjustment_factor=0.8 at lines 157-162. create_lifestyle_profile at lines 269-308.
_band_label_from_0_100 at lines 87-95: stable>=65, watch>=45. Metabolic domain at lines 455-488.

stage_2b.verdict: PASS — with CRITICAL implementation constraint on fix classification
stage_2b.behavioural_surface_area: OBS-2 root cause is in the questionnaire-to-scoring bridge. Any fix changes band labels for partial questionnaire inputs. No fix is zero-surface-area. The narrowest fix is in _map_exercise_minutes (mapper layer) — change return 0 to return None when no exercise fields present, then filter None from lifestyle_factors dict before injecting into user_data. This restores baseline-equivalence without affecting full questionnaire submissions.
stage_2b.expected_output_changes: If code fix is implemented: AB statin_off consumer_band_labels[1] changes from 'watch' back to 'stable' (matching baseline). VR statin_off bands would similarly equalise with VR baseline. statin_off vs statin_on invariant must remain PASS (both would have the fix applied identically). If no code fix: investigation note documents the behaviour as intentional and a test locks current band values.
regression_targets: AB statin_off vs AB statin_on: consumer_band_labels must remain identical (invariant PASS)
AB baseline vs AB statin_off: second band must match after fix (the fix target)
AB lifestyle_context: bands must remain unchanged — lifestyle comes from fixture not questionnaire
Full questionnaire submission: exercise_minutes_per_week must still score when exercise questions answered
VR baseline vs VR statin_off: band equivalence after fix
stage_2b.determinism_preserved: CONDITIONAL — preserved only if fix does not introduce any branch dependent on wall-clock time, random state, or LLM reasoning. mapper/orchestrator fix is deterministic by nature.
stage_2b.no_scope_creep: Guardrails explicitly prohibit broad questionnaire redesign and statin-path redesign. Fix must be confined to the exercise default sentinel at the mapper or orchestrator layer.
determinism_risks: Fix to _map_exercise_minutes must not use any sentinel that could be falsy (e.g. None) in a way that accidentally activates a 0 score elsewhere — test must confirm no accidental downstream effect.
If fix is at orchestrator layer (lifestyle_data gate), it must not suppress legitimate lifestyle data when exercise IS answered as part of a full questionnaire.
